scopeNote: Recovery-review is clean on the current propagated stack because every linked scrutiny review artifact now points at a commit id that both resolves and is ancestor-reachable from `recovery/08-engineer-shareout`; stale pre-restack anchors were replaced with reachable propagated equivalents so the restored pass state is auditable rather than a carried-forward clean summary from older ancestry.

appliedUpdates.description: Re-anchored every stale non-ancestor recovery-review artifact to a current-stack reachable commit id: O2C `140ae266b089673dbdbe724b60946827c6afffe9`, P2P `7b442c757a8cb4c55809d6235c56de18777506a0`, corrections/control `826fefe672f48752336dc043f27f9c173be8009f`, portal `ad58bbaa48fd7505c41167f85c1e7baaaf1ddce5`, final-hardening `f30d9bbabc5ad37137d4d601fad5c6e63b6b270a`, and the docs-only PR #102 shareout review `a16a5dac5ede13fdd7ba07ad1aecb9c5ad095f74`; the shared-ci baseline and truth-rails anchors were already reachable and remained unchanged. The milestone pass therefore reflects the live propagated ancestry rather than a stale clean summary from an older restack state.

Recorded the aggregate traceability verification result explicitly: all eight linked recovery-review artifacts now point at commitIds that both resolve and are branch-reachable on `recovery/08-engineer-shareout`, so the reported 8/8 pass count no longer overstates replayable evidence.

rejectedObservations.observation: The scrutiny-feature-reviewer Task droid failed to launch for every recovery-review feature with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-medium`, so all feature reviews were completed manually from mission handoffs, progress logs, and commit diffs.

The P2P review surfaced a runtime-probe contract mismatch between `.factory/services.yaml` and `testing/ci/test_pr_review_ci_packet.py`, but resolving it would require a non-additive manifest change outside this validator packet and outside the required recovery-review validator surface.
